message:
fix: accurate 2h window and error_detail in GA4 error monitor

* feat: add useRBACFindings hook with live cluster data (#3085, #3084, #3088)

- Create web/src/hooks/useRBACFindings.ts — fetches live RBAC data from
  connected clusters via kubectlProxy (clusterrolebindings, clusterroles,
  rolebindings). Analyzes for cluster-admin (critical), wildcard secrets
  (high), default SA escalation (high), wide read access (medium), and
  elevated namespace roles (low). Streams per-cluster results progressively,
  uses localStorage cache, auto-refresh every 5min, demo fallback.
- Update RBACExplorer.tsx to use useRBACFindings instead of the fake
  setTimeout demo simulation (#3084)
- Add STORAGE_KEY_RBAC_CACHE / _TIME constants to storage.ts
- Add web/e2e/RBACExplorer.spec.ts with E2E tests for demo mode
  (findings render, risk filter, search) and live mode (empty state,
  demo data guard) (#3088)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: increase stream gap threshold to reduce message fragmentation (#3071)

Increase STREAM_GAP_THRESHOLD_MS from 2000ms to 8000ms. At 2s, brief
pauses during tool execution (kubectl calls, permission checks) were
incorrectly creating new message bubbles. 8s better reflects the
typical latency of real tool-use gaps vs in-flight streaming.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: fix data race in runBuildCmd stdout/stderr capture

exec.Cmd copies stdout and stderr via separate goroutines. Using a plain
strings.Builder as the shared capture buffer was a data race — concurrent
Write calls could clobber each other, causing stderr to be silently
dropped from the combined output string.

Fix: introduce a writerFunc adapter that wraps writes to the builder in a
sync.Mutex, making concurrent stdout/stderr writes safe.

Fixes TestRunBuildCmd_OutputCapture: test now sees both stdout and stderr
in res.output as expected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: fix GA4 monitor time window and add error_detail to issues

The monitor claimed to look back 2h but actually queried '1daysAgo'
(up to 24h of data), making issue titles like '12 errors (last 2h)'
misleading. The low threshold of 5 was easily crossed by 24h
accumulations, creating noise for errors at an otherwise fine rate.

Fix the time window by:
- Adding dateHour dimension to the GA4 query
- Filtering rows client-side to the actual lookbackHours window
- Using a 14h buffer to account for property timezone offsets (±UTC)

Also add a second query to capture top error_detail values so future
GA4-error issues include the actual error messages, making them
immediately actionable without needing GA4 dashboard access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
